q: The _______ of a wave is the distance between two consecutive crests or troughs.
Sound waves are _______ waves because the particles of the medium vibrate parallel to the direction of energy transport.
The speed of sound in _______ is generally higher than in liquids or gases.
The frequency range of _______ sound is between 20 Hz and 20,000 Hz.
Sound waves with frequencies above 20,000 Hz are called _______.
Sound waves with frequencies below 20 Hz are called _______.
The speed of sound in air at 0°C is approximately _______ m/s.
The _______ of sound increases as the temperature of the air increases.
The change in observed frequency due to the relative motion of the source and observer is the _______ effect.
When a source of sound approaches a stationary observer, the observed frequency _______.
When a source of sound moves away from a stationary observer, the observed frequency _______.
An _______ is a reflected sound wave that arrives at the listener after a delay.
The _______ of a sound wave is determined by its frequency.
The _______ of sound depends on the amplitude of the vibration.
The unit used to measure sound intensity level is the _______.
The _______ frequency is the lowest frequency at which a system vibrates naturally.
Frequencies that are integral multiples of the fundamental frequency are called _______.
A _______ wave is formed by the superposition of two waves of the same frequency traveling in opposite directions.
In a standing wave, points of zero displacement are called _______.
In a standing wave, points of maximum displacement are called _______.
An _______ pipe has antinodes at both of its ends.
A pipe _______ at one end has a node at the closed end and an antinode at the open end.
A pipe closed at one end can only produce _______ harmonics.
The phenomena of _______ occurs when the frequency of a periodic force matches the natural frequency of a system.
_______ are periodic variations in sound intensity caused by the interference of two nearly equal frequencies.
The _______ number is the ratio of the speed of an object to the speed of sound in the medium.
A _______ boom is the loud noise heard when an object breaks the sound barrier.
_______ waves are conical waves produced by an object moving faster than sound.
The _______ of a sound allows us to distinguish between two sounds of the same pitch and loudness.
Ultrasonic waves have a frequency _______ than 20 kHz.
Medical imaging using ultrasound is commonly called _______.
Bats use _______ to navigate and catch prey in the dark.
The threshold of hearing corresponds to _______ decibels.
The distance between a node and the adjacent antinode is _______ of a wavelength.
The distance between two consecutive nodes in a standing wave is _______ of a wavelength.
_______ is the bending of sound waves around corners or obstacles.
The speed of sound is higher in _______ air than in dry air at the same temperature.
In a longitudinal wave, a _______ is a region where the medium is compressed.
In a longitudinal wave, a _______ is a region where the medium is spread out.
If an observer and source move in the same direction at the same speed, the Doppler shift is _______.
The number of beats per second is equal to the _______ in frequencies of the two sound sources.
The speed of sound in water is approximately _______ times faster than in air.
A _______ frequency is any frequency higher than the fundamental frequency.
The loudness level that causes physical pain to the human ear is around _______ dB.
Ultrasound can reveal _______ in blood flow within arteries.
The speed of a wave is the product of its frequency and its _______.
_______ is the objective measurement of the energy carried by a sound wave per unit area per unit time.
In an open pipe, the first overtone is the _______ harmonic.
In a closed pipe, the first overtone is the _______ harmonic.
A stationary observer perceives a _______ frequency when a source moves away.
